questions.question: Define overfitting in the context of machine learning and explain how gradient descent can lead to overfitting when training neural networks. Also, distinguish between underfitting, optimal fitting, and overfitting with respect to model complexity.
keyPoints: Overfitting occurs when model learns training data noise, performing well on training but poorly on validation/test data
Gradient descent with excessive epochs can cause model to memorize training data instead of learning generalizable patterns
Model complexity (too many parameters) combined with unlimited training leads to overfitting
Optimal fitting achieves good generalization; underfitting fails to capture data patterns

questions.question: Explain the concept of regularization in machine learning and describe how L1 (Lasso) and L2 (Ridge) regularization help prevent overfitting during gradient descent optimization. Provide the modified loss function for each type.
keyPoints: Regularization adds penalty term to loss function to constrain model parameters
L2 (Ridge): Adds λ||w||² to loss, shrinks weights proportionally, keeps all features
L1 (Lasso): Adds λ||w|| to loss, promotes sparsity by driving some weights to exactly zero
Gradient descent updates include regularization term: w = w - α(∂L/∂w + λw for L2)

questions.question: (a) Explain the bias-variance tradeoff and its relationship with overfitting. How does this tradeoff manifest when using gradient descent for training? (b) Discuss three techniques to prevent overfitting in neural networks trained with gradient descent, providing implementation details and code snippets for each technique. (c) How does dropout work as a regularization technique and what is the intuition behind it?
keyPoints: High bias causes underfitting (oversimplified model), high variance causes overfitting (complex model); tradeoff balances二者
Techniques: (i) L2 regularization with weight decay, (ii) Dropout during training, (iii) Early stopping based on validation loss
Dropout randomly deactivates neurons during forward pass, forcing network to learn redundant representations
Early stopping monitors validation loss, stops training when it starts increasing while training loss decreases

questions.question: (a) With the help of a diagram (describe it in text), illustrate the training loss and validation loss curves that demonstrate overfitting. Label the key regions: underfitting zone, optimal stopping point, and overfitting zone. (b) Write complete Python code using a deep learning framework (TensorFlow/Keras or PyTorch) to demonstrate early stopping as a technique to prevent overfitting during gradient descent training. Include data loading, model creation, training with early stopping callback, and plotting of training/validation curves. (c) Explain how the learning rate affects overfitting and discuss adaptive learning rate optimizers.
keyPoints: Diagram shows training loss continuously decreasing while validation loss decreases then increases after optimal point
Early stopping callback monitors validation_loss with patience parameter, restores best weights on stop
Code should include: model.fit() with validation_data, EarlyStopping callback, ModelCheckpoint for saving best model
High learning rate can cause oscillation and poor convergence; low learning rate leads to slow convergence; Adam/RMSprop provide adaptive learning rates
